response:
To find the **greatest common divisor (GCD)** of two numbers, we can use the **Euclidean algorithm**.

---

### Step 1: Apply the Euclidean Algorithm

We are given:
$$
a = 106029,\quad b = 38115
$$

We want to find:
$$
\gcd(106029, 38115)
$$

#### Step 1.1: Divide the larger number by the smaller one
$$
106029 \div 38115 = 2 \quad \text{remainder} \quad 106029 - 2 \times 38115 = 106029 - 76230 = 29799
$$

So:
$$
\gcd(106029, 38115) = \gcd(38115, 29799)
$$

#### Step 1.2: Repeat the process
$$
38115 \div 29799 = 1 \quad \text{remainder} \quad 38115 - 29799 = 8316
$$

So:
$$
\gcd(38115, 29799) = \gcd(29799, 8316)
$$

#### Step 1.3: Continue
$$
29799 \div 8316 = 3 \quad \text{remainder} \quad 29799 - 3 \times 8316 = 29799 - 24948 = 4851
$$

So:
$$
\gcd(29799, 8316) = \gcd(8316, 4851)
$$

#### Step 1.4: Continue
$$
8316 \div 4851 = 1 \quad \text{remainder} \quad 8316 - 4851 = 3465
$$

So:
$$
